title: Rocket Lab Just Signed the Biggest Launch Contract in the Company's History. But Is the Stock a Buy in 2026?
text: Rocket Lab achieved a significant milestone by securing its largest launch contract ever with an undisclosed customer. The agreement encompasses multiple missions using both the company's Neutron and Electron rockets. During Q1 2026, Rocket Lab demonstrated exceptional momentum, signing 31 launch contracts—surpassing the entire total from 2025. The company now maintains a $2.2 billion backlog with over 70 launch contracts on its manifest. This expansion reflects growing government and commercial demand for private space services. In addition to the record contract, Rocket Lab agreed to acquire Motiv Space Systems, a California-based robotics specialist. Management projects Q2 revenue between $225-240 million, representing roughly 60% year-over-year growth. While Rocket Lab remains unprofitable, analysts anticipate the company may break even, or come close to it, next year, as revenue scales from increased launch activities and expanded capabilities. The organization continues building critical infrastructure supporting planetary exploration and national security missions.
